issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query removes the outline without providing a visible alternative, making focus invisible for users in high-contrast mode. The outline-offset property alone does not render a visible ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset, which does not create a visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus. This affects all interactive elements that rely on the default focus style.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em, which compresses text and reduces readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible removes the outline and only sets outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard users navigating dropdown menus.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width/height: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Screen-reader-only text remains visible to sighted users, cluttering the visual interface.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the transform animation for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small button variant
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this small target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 428: min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on statistics labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label uses color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (30% white) against a gradient background of #5588bb to #bb88ee, resulting in a contrast ratio below 4.5:1. Users with low vision cannot read the labels.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing a non-colour visual differentiator for links at rest state. Inline links in body text rely solely on colour, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color for users who cannot perceive colour differences.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on navigation toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle class sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible selector removes the outline and sets outline-offset to 0 without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border, leaving no focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible replacement, relying only on outline-offset which renders nothing.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector removes the outline and only provides outline-offset, which does not create a visible focus ring for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on feature card links
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector removes the outline and only sets outline-offset, failing to provide a visible focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on FAQ buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector removes the outline and only provides outline-offset, leaving no visible indication of focus for keyboard users expanding FAQ items.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on text inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input:focus-visible selector removes the outline and sets outline-offset to 0 without providing a visible alternative focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on select elements
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group select:focus-visible selector removes the outline and sets outline-offset to 0 without providing a visible alternative focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on textareas
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group textarea:focus-visible selector removes the outline and sets outline-offset to 0 without providing a visible alternative focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible selector removes the outline and only provides outline-offset, failing to show focus on checkbox controls.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:focus-visible selector removes the outline and only sets outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for social media links.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer brand links
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand a:focus-visible selector removes the outline and only sets outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible selector removes the outline and only provides outline-offset, leaving footer links without visible focus.

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, creating a touch target smaller than the 24px minimum required for accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required for pointer accuracy.
issuesFound.fix: Line 473: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 486: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is 28px × 28px with no padding, below the 44px recommended touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 613: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is 20px × 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size for pointer accuracy.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1331: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer nav links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have min-height: 24px with no padding, falling short of the 44px recommended target size without compensation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1358: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; padding: 10px 0; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and impairs readability for users with cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 295: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative in resting state
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration without providing a non-colour differentiator (like underline or border) in the resting state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 328: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn component uses transform transitions but lacks a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 863: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform transition but lacks a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 673: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid and markValid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Form validation helpers update aria-describedby to link error messages but fail to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or aria-invalid="false" on valid fields. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state of form controls.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1233: After `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`
  Line ~1252: After the if block closing brace, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded state updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are shown and hidden using the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but the trigger buttons do not have their aria-expanded attributes updated. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded/collapsed state of the dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2908: After `inst.open = true;` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line ~2921: After `inst.open = false;` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a `_trap` variable for focus trapping and deactivates it on close, but never initializes or activates the focus trap when the menu opens. Keyboard users can tab out of the mobile menu into the background page.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The open and close functions accept an `announceToSR` parameter but never use it to announce state changes via the live region. Screen reader users are not notified when accordion items expand or collapse.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded management
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but never initializes or updates aria-expanded to reflect whether the suggestion listbox is visible. Screen readers cannot convey the open/closed state of the suggestions dropdown.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~3527: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');` add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`
  Line ~3622: After `_searchState.open = true;` add `if (_inputEl) setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line ~3632: After `_searchState.open = false;` add `if (_inputEl) setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates and the count badge changes, but no announcement is made to screen readers about the number of results shown or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~4455: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` add:
  ```
  liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found for current filter`);
  ```

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (Monthly/Quarterly/Annual) visually indicate the active state via CSS classes but lack aria-pressed attributes. Screen readers cannot identify which period is currently selected in this mutually-exclusive button group.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~4209: After `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly, quarterly, and annual billing, the price values update visually with animation, but screen readers are not informed of the price change or the new billing period selection.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~4180: After `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });` add:
  ```
  liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);
  ```

issuesFound.title: siteSearch initialization missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When mounting the suggestion listbox, the combobox input receives role, aria-haspopup, and aria-controls, but lacks the required aria-expanded="false" initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3527: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');` add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll-to-top button is activated, the page scrolls to the top and focus is moved, but no announcement is made to screen readers confirming the action occurred.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~3130: After `setTimeout(() => { returnEl.focus({ preventScroll: true }); }, 300);` add:
  ```
  liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');
  ```

issuesFound.title: mobileNav initialization missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is set up with event listeners, but aria-expanded is never initialized to reflect the closed state on page load.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2870: After `_trigger.addEventListener('click', _onTriggerClick);` add:
  ```
  if (!_trigger.getAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
  ```

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu closes (via Escape, outside click, or toggle), focus is not returned to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place in the tab order.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2840: Before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });` add:
  ```
  if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();
  ```

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu close does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdowns close via outside click or Escape, the `_closeInstance` function accepts a `returnFocus` parameter but callers do not pass `true`, leaving focus stranded or moved to the body.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2932: In `_onDocumentClick`, change `_closeAll();` to `_closeAll(null, true);` (or ensure focus returns to the trigger that was open)

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion announceToSR parameter unused
issuesFound.explanation: Both functions declare an `announceToSR` parameter intended to control screen reader announcements, but the parameter is never referenced in the function bodies, preventing conditional announcement logic from working.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~5010: Change function body to use the parameter as shown in Issue 8 fix above, or remove the parameter if not needed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function adds the `is-open` class to the drawer and body but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the `_trigger` button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1162: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close` function removes the `is-open` class but fails to set `aria-expanded="false"` on the `_trigger` button. The ARIA state remains out of sync with the visual state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1182: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function creates a focus trap instance (`_trap`) in `init` but never calls `_trap.activate()` when the menu opens. Keyboard focus can escape the modal drawer to the underlying page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1163: if (_trap) _trap.activate();

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions change significant UI state (menu visibility) but do not call `liveRegion.announce` with `STRINGS.announce.navOpen` or `navClosed`. Screen reader users are not notified of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1164: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);
  Line 1183: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function removes the `hidden` attribute from the panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on `inst.trigger`. The button state does not reflect the open panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1260: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function adds the `hidden` attribute to the panel but does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on `inst.trigger`. The button state remains "expanded" visually and semantically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1278: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` function expands the panel and sets `item.open = true`, but unlike the `close` function, it does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on `item.trigger`. This creates asymmetric state management.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2385: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` and `close` functions toggle answer visibility but do not call `liveRegion.announce` using `STRINGS.announce.faqOpened` or `faqClosed`. Users are not informed which question was expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2392: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question));
  Line 2415: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question));

issuesFound.title: pricing _syncToggleState does not update aria-checked on switch
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncToggleState` function toggles the `is-checked` CSS class on `_toggleEl` (which has `role="switch"`) but does not update the `aria-checked` attribute. The switch state is not exposed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2735: setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing.setPeriod` function updates prices and toggle state but does not call `liveRegion.announce` with `STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly` or `pricingAnnual`. Users are not notified of the billing context change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2705: liveRegion.announce(period === 'month' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly : STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual);

issuesFound.title: productFilter _syncTabAriaStates does not update aria-pressed on tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncTabAriaStates` function toggles the `is-active` class on filter buttons but does not update `aria-pressed`. These buttons act as a segmented control (radio group) and require `aria-pressed` to indicate selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3030: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: productFilter results update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter.applyFilters` function updates the product grid and count badge but does not call `liveRegion.announce` with the new count (e.g., "5 products found"). Screen reader users must manually explore to find results changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2955: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory));

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open function does not update aria-expanded on input
issuesFound.explanation: The `siteSearch._open` function sets `_searchState.open = true` and shows suggestions but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on `_inputEl` (which has `role="combobox"`). The expanded state is not communicated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3430: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _close function does not update aria-expanded on input
issuesFound.explanation: The `siteSearch._close` function hides suggestions but does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on `_inputEl`. The combobox remains semantically expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3445: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid` function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field itself. Assistive technologies rely on `aria-invalid` to announce the field is erroneous.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1665: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator _renderResult does not clear aria-invalid on valid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function calls `srUtils.markValid` which removes `aria-describedby` references but does not explicitly remove `aria-invalid` or set it to "false" when the field becomes valid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1530: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm submission errors not announced assertively
issuesFound.explanation: The `newsletterForm._showError` function updates the status element text but relies on the element's `role="alert"` (which is set). However, if the element was hidden, simply removing `hidden` might not trigger an announcement in all AT without a DOM change or focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1835: liveRegion.announceAssertive(msg);

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard step change announcement missing step title
issuesFound.explanation: The `_goToStep` function announces "Step X of Y" but the `STRINGS.wizardStepChanged` template includes `{title}` which is passed. Ensure `entering.title` is always defined. If `data-step-title` is missing, announcement is vague.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2140: const title = entering.title || `Step ${_current + 1}`;

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button aria-pressed not initialised
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel.init` function sets up the play button but does not set the initial `aria-pressed` state based on whether auto-play is active by default. It relies on `play()` being called to set it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2535: setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(CONFIG.carousel && CONFIG.carousel.autoPlay !== false));

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner focus trap not released on accept/decline
issuesFound.explanation: The `_hideBanner` function calls `_trapRelease()` which is good. However, `_onAccept` and `_onDecline` call `_hideBanner`. Ensure `_trapRelease` is defined before these are called. In `init`, `_trapRelease` is assigned in `_showBanner`. If banner is already shown, this is fine. If logic changes, risk exists.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1770: if (_trapRelease) { _trapRelease(); _trapRelease = null; }

issuesFound.title: viewportReveal does not respect aria-hidden for decorative reveals
issuesFound.explanation: The `viewportReveal._reveal` function adds `is-revealed` class. If the element was `aria-hidden="true"` for performance before reveal, it should be removed. Currently it only removes `data-reveal`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2288: el.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator does not announce final value
issuesFound.explanation: The `statCounterAnimator._animate` function updates `textContent` continuously. Screen readers may try to read every intermediate number. It should ideally only announce the final value or use `aria-valuenow` on a progress-like element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2342: el.setAttribute('aria-valuenow', String(cfg.target));

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown decorative chevron not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon indicating submenu state is decorative but lacks aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce the triangle character as content. Decorative icons should be hidden from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1085: {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required field marker not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk marker indicating required fields is not marked with aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce "star" as part of the label text, which is redundant with the visually hidden "(required)" text that follows.
issuesFound.fix: Line 890: *

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The skip links container is a nav element without an aria-label, making it indistinguishable from other navigation landmarks when multiple nav elements exist on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 660: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button renders only an emoji icon without an aria-label, leaving screen reader users with no indication of the button's purpose. The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240: <IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle" aria-label={LABELS[theme]}>

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation uses a nav element without an aria-label, preventing screen reader users from distinguishing it from other navigation regions on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1060: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Desktop navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link for the current page has a visual indicator via CSS class but lacks aria-current="page", preventing screen readers from announcing the current page location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1120: {item.label}

issuesFound.title: HeroSection region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section uses a section element without aria-labelledby or aria-label, failing to create a labelled landmark for the primary content region despite having a visible heading.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1380: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar cloned list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated integration list used for visual marquee looping lacks aria-hidden, causing screen readers to encounter duplicate content and repetitive announcements of the same integration partners.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1530: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading prop to show a spinner but does not set aria-busy on the button element. Screen reader users are not informed that the button is processing and unavailable for interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 825: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that expands and collapses the mobile navigation sections has aria-controls but no aria-expanded attribute. This prevents screen readers from announcing the current expanded state of the accordion panels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel previous button contains only a character entity without visible text or aria-label, making it inaccessible to screen reader users. The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2160: <IconButton aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev" aria-label="Previous testimonial"> ‹ </IconButton>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel next button contains only a character entity without visible text or aria-label, making it inaccessible to screen reader users. The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2165: <IconButton aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next" aria-label="Next testimonial"> › </IconButton>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel contains a nav element without an aria-label, making it impossible for screen reader users to distinguish from the main site navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1140: <nav className="mobile-nav__body" aria-label="Mobile navigation">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics bar uses a section element without aria-labelledby or aria-label, missing the opportunity to create a labelled landmark for the social proof content despite having a visible heading.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1480: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan card uses an article element without aria-labelledby, preventing screen reader users from identifying the specific plan when navigating by landmarks. The card contains a heading with id that should be referenced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1970: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation link for the current page has a visual indicator via CSS class but lacks aria-current="page", preventing screen readers from announcing the current page location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1170: {item.label}

issuesFound.title: StarRating individual stars not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star characters inside the rating component are decorative but lack aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce each star character separately in addition to the container's aria-label that already conveys the rating value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 670: {stars.map((filled, i) => ( {filled ? '★' : '☆'} ))}

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel non-visible slides missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel slides that are not currently visible remain in the accessibility tree without aria-hidden, allowing screen reader users to navigate to and read content from hidden slides, creating confusion about the page structure.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2120: <div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: Section component ignores labelledby prop
issuesFound.explanation: The Section component accepts a labelledby prop but does not apply it to the rendered element as aria-labelledby, preventing the creation of labelled landmarks when the component is used with this prop.
issuesFound.fix: Line 920: <Tag id={id} aria-labelledby={labelledby} className={['page-section', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div with onClick missing button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop element uses a div with an onClick handler to close the navigation but lacks role="button", tabindex, and keyboard event handlers. Keyboard users cannot dismiss the mobile navigation by activating the backdrop.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1130: <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose} role="button" aria-label="Close navigation" tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }} />

issuesFound.title: Skip navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> element wrapping the skip links lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot distinguish this navigation region from others on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 506: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: Star rating individual icons missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star spans inside StarRating are not hidden from assistive technology, even though the container has an aria-label. This causes screen readers to announce each star individually in addition to the container label, creating redundancy and noise.
issuesFound.fix: Line 532: <span key={i} aria-hidden="true" className={filled ? 'star star--filled' : 'star star--empty'}>

issuesFound.title: Reusable Button component missing aria-busy state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading prop but does not pass aria-busy={isLoading} to the underlying <button> element. Assistive technology users are not informed when the button is in a busy loading state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 563: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-busy={isLoading} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined}

issuesFound.title: Required field marker announces asterisk to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk indicating a required field is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers will announce "asterisk" or "star" unnecessarily, as the required state is already conveyed by the input's required attribute and aria-required.
issuesFound.fix: Line 593: *

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown decorative chevron missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron span indicating dropdown state is decorative but lacks aria-hidden="true". It may be announced by screen readers as text content, adding noise to the navigation link label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 640: <span aria-hidden="true" className="nav-link__chevron">

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link indicating the current page uses a visual class (nav-link--current) but lacks the aria-current="page" attribute. Screen reader users are not informed that this link represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 665: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation body landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> element inside the mobile panel lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. Users navigating by landmarks cannot identify the purpose of this specific navigation region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 729: <nav aria-label="Mobile navigation" className="mobile-nav__body">

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation link indicating the current page uses a visual class but lacks the aria-current="page" attribute. Screen reader users are not informed that this link represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 753: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}

issuesFound.title: Hero section landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <section> element for the hero area lacks an aria-labelledby attribute referencing its h1 heading. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot identify this section's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 893: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: Hero video toggle button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggling the background video play state is a two-state toggle but lacks aria-pressed. While aria-label changes, aria-pressed is the standard ARIA property for communicating toggle button state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 925: <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" aria-pressed={isPlaying} aria-label={isPlaying ? 'Pause background video' : 'Play background video'}

issuesFound.title: Stats section landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <section> element for statistics lacks an aria-labelledby attribute referencing its h2 heading (which is visually hidden). Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot identify this section's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 967: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan card article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <article> element for the pricing plan lacks an aria-labelledby attribute referencing its h3 heading. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks or articles cannot identify the specific plan name associated with this card.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1405: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan CTA link missing plan name in accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The CTA link text (e.g., "Get started for free") does not include the specific plan name (e.g., "Starter"). When multiple such links exist on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish which plan they are selecting.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1438: <a href={plan.ctaHref} aria-label={`${plan.ctaLabel} — ${plan.name} plan`} className={[
